publication_safe_tension: Library visibility plus later route failure does not prove bytes are absent or non-materialisable through every route.
Eleven recorded targets and a 31-row retrospective inventory do not supply a population denominator or independent conditions.
resolution_or_ceiling: Do not publish a failure rate or prevalence.

publication_safe_tension: Generated versus uploaded differences can be confounded by name session suffix upload and lineage history.
resolution_or_ceiling: Treat origin/object-state association as descriptive; do not name a component.

publication_safe_tension: The controlled ZIP current bytes match a prior recorded uploaded hash, but separately preserved uploaded bytes and origin metadata are absent.
resolution_or_ceiling: Publish current-byte result with first-party provenance ceiling.

publication_safe_tension: Rename-and-reupload succeeded as a sequence but did not prove namespace eviction or repair of the original object.
A search row or reference does not establish exact target identity.
resolution_or_ceiling: Do not equate semantic retrieval with object resolution.

publication_safe_tension: Rendered-versus-extracted PDF differences do not identify a parser font map or encoding cause.
A Gmail reconnect surface does not establish Gmail search read draft send or invocation.
resolution_or_ceiling: Do not state a connector action.

publication_safe_tension: Progress wording can outrun later disclosed state without revealing hidden tool state or background work.
resolution_or_ceiling: Treat narration as a claim requiring execution receipts.

publication_safe_tension: Discrete public file incidents do not establish one cause for all anomalies.
